title: ‘Praised Mamata Banerjee’s sarees, never raised complaints’: TMC slams rebels
summary:
- Mamata Banerjee-led Trinamool Congress is facing a major crisis with a rebel group led by Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar claiming to have the support of 20 Lok Sabha MPs, a claim contested by the party leadership.
- The rebel group met Union Minister Bhupender Yadav and Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari on Monday, and Kalyan Banerjee, a Trinamool MP, said these leaders have effectively become BJP.
- The Trinamool Congress claims that the rebel MPs who have defected have never raised any serious complaints against the leadership and have instead praised Mamata Banerjee's leadership and development work.
- The anti-defection law may not apply if the rebel group has the support of 20 MPs, as it claims, and Kalyan Banerjee has challenged the rebel MPs to go to their constituencies and meet party workers who campaigned for them.

title: Zojila tunnel hits breakthrough stage, Nitin Gadkari highlights strategic importance
summary:
- The National Highways & Infrastructure Development Corporation Limited (NHIDCL) has achieved a breakthrough in the ambitious Zojila tunnel project on the Sonmarg-Kargil section of National Highway-01 (NH-01) in Jammu & Kashmir.
- The Zojila tunnel, one of the most difficult road projects in India, is scheduled to be completed by February 2028 and will provide all-weather connectivity between Sonamarg (J&K) and Minamarg (Ladakh).
- The total cost of the Zojila tunnel project and the road from Z-Morh tunnel to the Zojila tunnel is around Rs. 7000 crore, and the tunnel will reduce the travel time between Sonamarg-Minamarg from 1.5 hours to 45 minutes.
- As of 2026, a total of 54 tunnels have been built across the country at a cost of Rs. 20,000 crore in the last 11 years, and currently, the work is underway on 128 tunnels at a cost of Rs. 1.12 lakh crore.

title: Twisha case: ‘VIP treatment’ for mother-in-law in prison prompts action
summary:
- The Twisha Sharma death case involves Giribala Singh, a retired district judge, and her son Samarth Singh, who were sent to 14 days' judicial custody after the CBI completed its custodial questioning.
- The case has drawn scrutiny due to reports of preferential treatment extended to Giribala Singh, including being housed in a separate ward and being served special meals.
- Giribala Singh is now subject to the same daily schedule as other inmates, with a standard daily routine that includes morning prayers, tea, breakfast, lunch, evening tea, and dinner.
- The incident has raised questions about the treatment of high-profile inmates in Indian prisons, with the Bhopal Jail Superintendent stating that Giribala Singh was not provided any preferential treatment.

title: Congress’s ‘Aurangzeb’ jibe at Rajasthan CM over shifting of temples
summary:
- The Rajasthan Chief Minister Bhajan Lal Sharma's government has been likened to Mughal ruler Aurangzeb by the Congress party after two temples were shifted as part of an anti-encroachment drive by the Jaipur Development Authority (JDA) on Monday.
- Between 2012 and 2015, the Vasundhara Raje-led BJP government removed or shifted 93 religious structures, including temples and some mazaars, in Jaipur alone, primarily due to obstruction to Metro work and transportation, or illegal construction or encroachment on government land.
- In 2015, the RSS called the Raje government's attitude "worse than that of Mughal Emperor Aurangzeb" and summoned nine BJP MLAs to its headquarters in Bharati Bhawan in Jaipur, asking them to explain their "inaction" over the demolition of temples.
- In April 2022, the Ashok Gehlot-led Congress government faced a massive political backlash after a 300-year-old Shiva temple in Alwar's Rajgarh was demolished as part of an anti-encroachment drive.

title: ED summons Kerala ex-CM Pinarayi Vijayan’s daughter in money laundering probe
summary:
- The Enforcement Directorate (ED) has summoned Veena Vijayan, daughter of former Kerala Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan, for questioning on June 12 in connection with a money laundering investigation linked to Cochin Minerals and Rutile Limited (CMRL).
- The ED alleges that CMRL made payments amounting to Rs 2.78 crore to Exalogic Solutions Pvt Ltd, a now-defunct IT firm owned by Veena Vijayan, under the guise of IT consultancy services.
- The Serious Fraud Investigation Office (SFIO) had earlier examined the financial relationship between Exalogic and CMRL, and found that Exalogic received a monthly consultancy fee of Rs 8 lakh from CMRL, with little evidence of actual consultancy work being carried out.

title: Parliamentary panel seeks views on bill to tweak corporate laws
summary:
- The Companies Act, 2013, and the Limited Liability Partnership (LLP) Act, 2008, are to undergo comprehensive amendments as per the proposed legislation.
- The bill was introduced in March this year, but the exact date is not specified in the article.
- The parliamentary panel has sought views on the bill to tweak corporate laws, but the name of the panel or its chairman is not mentioned in the article.
- The exact details of the proposed amendments are not specified in the article.

title: DSS to hold rallies on June 16 seeking Karnataka Cabinet berth for H.C. Mahadevappa
summary:
- The Karnataka State Dalit Sangharsha Samiti has announced a statewide rally on June 16.
- The rally is demanding a Cabinet berth for H.C. Mahadevappa, a senior Congress leader and former Minister, in the proposed expansion of the State Cabinet.
- No other relevant information for competitive exams is available in the article.

title: Pawan Kalyan questions VSP safety after blast, flags similar incident the night before
summary:
Here are the key points from the news article for SSC Exam preparation:

- Pawan Kalyan, Deputy Chief Minister of Andhra Pradesh, expressed concerns over the safety of Visakhapatnam Steel Plant (VSP) after a blast occurred.
- He also mentioned that a similar incident occurred the night before the reported blast, indicating potential safety issues at the plant.
- Pawan Kalyan emphasized the need for upgradation of VSP and criticized the delay in allotment of captive mines for the plant.

title: Former TMC MLA and first mayor of Bidhnanagar held by Kolkata police
summary:
- Sabyasachi Dutta, former Trinamool Congress MLA and first mayor of Bidhannagar Municipal Corporation, was arrested on June 8, 2026, for allegedly extorting over ₹1.05 crore from a Salt Lake-based businessman in 2018.
- Dutta was fielded by the Trinamool Congress in the recent state assembly election from the Barasat constituency but lost to BJP's Sankar Chatterjee.
- Dutta had previously switched to the BJP in October 2019, along with eight other Trinamool Congress MLAs, but returned to the Trinamool Congress in 2021 ahead of the State assembly election.

title: Indian sailor’s last call with family: ‘We got two warnings from the US Navy’
summary:
- The Indian seafarer, Aditya Sharma, 23, was killed in an attack by American forces on MT Settebello off the coast of Oman on June 9, 2026.
- The US military said it attacked the tanker after it tried to "violate" the blockade put up by the US outside the Strait of Hormuz, off the Oman coast.
- Union Minister of Ports, Shipping and Waterways Sarbananda Sonowal confirmed the deaths of the three Indian seafarers who were reported missing and directed officials to ensure immediate repatriation of the rescued crew members and swift return of the mortal remains of the deceased.

title: 5 Indians rescued after 3rd ship on fire off Oman; 3 dead in last US strike
summary:
- The incident involved a vessel, MT Jalveer, off the coast of Oman, where 5 Indian crew members were rescued.
- The Indian Embassy in Oman is closely monitoring the situation and coordinating with local authorities for further details.
- Three Indian nationals were killed in a US strike on another oil tanker, MT Settebello, which was transporting oil from Iran.
- Another ship, MT Marivex, with Indian crew members, was attacked on Monday while attempting to dock at an Iranian port.

title: Wanted in murder, extortion cases, Haryana gangster Venkat Garg extradited from Georgia
summary:
- The Haryana gangster Venkat Garg was extradited from Georgia to India on Thursday, wanted in multiple cases of murder, attempt to murder, extortion, organised crime, and illegal possession and use of firearms in Haryana.
- India doesn't have an extradition treaty with Georgia, and the process of getting Garg back took over a year, with a joint declaration on reciprocity towards mutual legal assistance signed between the two sides in the absence of an extradition treaty.
- A Red Notice was issued against Garg through Interpol at the request of the Haryana Police, and his passports were revoked along with those of nine other criminals by the Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) at the request of the Delhi Police.
- This is the first extradition of a criminal wanted by Indian authorities from Georgia, and India and Georgia are now working towards signing an extradition treaty, the discussions for which have been going on since last year.

title: Kerala reports suspected Nipah virus case — trader whose godown had fruit bats
summary:
- The first suspected Nipah virus case in Kerala this year has been reported in Kozhikode, with a 43-year-old trader admitted to an isolation ward at the Government Medical College Hospital.

- The patient, who had developed symptoms of the infection a few days ago, had a godown close to thick vegetation where a large number of fruit bats were found, which are considered the reservoir of the virus.

- The Kerala Health Department has activated all protocols for dealing with a Nipah outbreak, including declaring a containment zone and isolating primary contacts of the patient, and advised people in Kozhikode's Ramanattukara municipality to practice social distancing and wear masks at public places.

- Kerala has reported a total of 24 deaths due to the Nipah virus since 2018, with most outbreaks occurring during the May to September period, which encompasses the main rainy season in the state.

title: Trinamool in tatters: Another MP resigns from Rajya Sabha, 3rd in 4 days
summary:
- The Trinamool Congress has lost three Rajya Sabha MPs in four days, with Prakash Chik Baraik being the latest to resign.
- The party now has 10 MPs in the Rajya Sabha, down from 13, and at least three more MPs are likely to resign in the coming days.
- The resignations come after the Trinamool Congress's defeat in the West Bengal Assembly polls last month, with the BJP winning a huge mandate in the state.

title: Nipah virus: Here’s what you need to know
summary:
Here are the key points from the news article relevant to the SSC Exam:

- Kerala has previously reported cases of the Nipah virus. 
- The spread of the virus can be curbed by strictly following guidelines issued by health authorities.

title: Digital arrest fraud: Bengaluru homemaker duped of ₹98.4 lakh during two-month ordeal
summary:
- The incident involves a homemaker from Bengaluru who was allegedly duped of ₹98.4 lakh through a digital arrest fraud.
- The fraudsters claimed that her identity documents had been misused in a money laundering case, and multiple criminal cases had been registered against her.
- The ordeal lasted for two months, during which the victim was threatened and coerced into paying the demanded amount.
- No specific details about the ministry or authorities involved in the case are mentioned in the article.

title: Kerala rains: Orange alert for two districts on June 12
summary:
- The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued an orange alert warning of very heavy rains for Idukki and Ernakulam on Friday (June 12, 2026).
- The districts of Pathanamthitta, Alappuzha, Kottayam, Thrissur, Kannur, and Kasaragod have been put on yellow alert, where isolated heavy rain is likely.
- The coastal areas of Kerala are likely to experience the 'kallakkadal' phenomenon – the sudden swell of the seas causing rough waves – from Thursday 8.30 p.m. to Friday 11.30 p.m.
- The high waves are expected to be between 0.8 to 1.4 meters along the coast of Kerala during this period.

title: Signature forgery case: Calcutta High Court directs Abhishek Banerjee to appear before CID
summary:
- The Calcutta High Court directed TMC leader Abhishek Banerjee to appear before the CID for investigation in a signature forgery case.
- The HC granted an interim protection to Abhishek Banerjee, preventing the CID from taking any coercive action against him as long as he cooperates with the investigation.
- No specific date for Abhishek Banerjee's appearance before the CID was mentioned in the news article.

title: Odisha govt. alleges two inquiry reports went missing from CMO during Naveen Patnaik tenure
summary:
- The Odisha government has alleged that two inquiry reports went missing from the Chief Minister's Office (CMO) during the tenure of Naveen Patnaik.
- The missing reports pertain to the Swami Laxmanananda Saraswati killing incident and the SUM Hospital fire incident.
- The Home Department has sought an FIR (First Information Report) in connection with the disappearance of these reports from the CMO.

title: Bitcoin scam: Congress MLA’s son in SIT chargesheet, Karnataka Home Minister says names of BJP leaders too will emerge
summary:
- Mohammed Haris Nalapad, son of Congress MLA N. A. Haris, has been named as a co-accused in the Karnataka Bitcoin scam chargesheet by the Special Investigation Team (SIT).
- The scam occurred during the tenure of the BJP government in Karnataka, and the Congress had alleged the involvement of senior BJP leaders ahead of the 2023 assembly polls.
- The stolen Bitcoins, valued at ₹1.14 crore in 2017, are now estimated to be worth over ₹35 crore.
- The Enforcement Directorate (ED) is carrying out its investigation under the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA), 2002.

title: Kerala HC grants State two weeks to respond to charges against Waqf Board in PIL by BJP’s Shone George
summary:
- The Kerala High Court has given the State two weeks to respond to charges against the Kerala Waqf Board in a Public Interest Litigation (PIL) filed by BJP's Shone George.
- The charges against the Kerala Waqf Board are related to its functioning in violation of mandates under the United Waqf Management, Empowerment, Efficiency and Development Act.
- The PIL was filed by Shone George, a BJP leader.

title: Homemakers are ‘nation builders’, their work worth at least ₹30,000 a month: Supreme Court
summary:
- The Supreme Court on June 11, 2026, ruled that homemakers deserve to be recognized as "nation builders" and their unpaid domestic work must be monetized at a minimum of ₹30,000 per month while calculating compensation for their deaths in road mishaps.
- The court observed that homemakers contribute to the growth of the human being and the nation, and their unpaid care giving work is estimated to contribute 15-17% of India's GDP.
- The court also noted the need to shift from the stereotypical terminology of daily usage from 'housewife' to 'homemaker', and that women aged between 15-59 years spend over seven hours daily on unpaid domestic tasks, compared to less than three hours by men.
- The court directed the Motor Accidents Claim Tribunal (MACT) to award a separate compensation of ₹30,000 per month under the head of 'domestic care' in cases involving death of a homemaker, which would increase by 10% every three years.

title: Supreme Court to hear Meenakshi Natarajan’s plea against rejection of RS candidature on June 12
summary:
- The Supreme Court will hear Meenakshi Natarajan's plea against the rejection of her Rajya Sabha (RS) candidature on June 12.
- The Supreme Court has declined to grant any interim relief preventing the declaration of the election result.
- There is no mention of any specific ministry or international treaty in this news article.
